chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C. P No.S-194 of 2025 Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objections ‘A’. 2. For orders on M.A NO.453/25 (E/A). 3. For hearing of main case. 20-10-2025 Mr. Riaz Hussain Hisbani, Advocate a/w petitioner. Mr. Aftab Nek Muhammad Bhutto, Assistant A.G. ************** Mr. Riaz Hussain Hisbani, Advocate, files Vakalatnama on behalf of the petitioner, which is hereby taken on record. Learned counsel for the petitioner contends that the official respondents are allegedly harassing the petitioner at the behest of other official respondents. He further submits that an application was earlier filed before the Justice of Peace, Kashmore at Kandhkot, which was disposed of with directions to the official respondents to provide protection to the petitioner. However, despite such directions, no effective relief has been provided. He, therefore, prays for appropriate directions to be issued to the official respondents to ensure protection for the petitioner. The learned Assistant Advocate General, present in Court, waives notice and submits that the official respondents are duty-bound to act strictly in accordance with law and shall provide protection to the petitioner, if and when required. Upon perusal of the petition, it appears that the underlying dispute between the parties is of a civil nature. It is a settled principle that this Court, in its constitutional jurisdiction, is not empowered to adjudicate upon disputed questions of fact or enter into civil controversies between private individuals. The petitioner and/or private respondents, if aggrieved by any act or omission arising out of such civil dispute, may seek appropriate remedy before the competent forum, in accordance with law.

It is a settled principle that this Court, in its constitutional jurisdiction, is not empowered to adjudicate upon disputed questions of fact or enter into civil controversies between private individuals. The petitioner and/or private respondents, if aggrieved by any act or omission arising out of such civil dispute, may seek appropriate remedy before the competent forum, in accordance with law. In view of the above, the instant petition is disposed of with directions to the official respondents to act strictly in accordance with law and to ensure that no harassment is caused to the petitioner, and to provide protection to him as and when required. Let a copy of this order be transmitted to the learned Additional Advocate General, Sindh, for information and necessary compliance. Judge Irshad Ali M/Steno
